IGF::OT::IGF  THE GOAL OF THIS FIVE YEAR NEPAL PUBLIC FINANCIAL MANAGEMENT STRENGTHENING PROJECT TO ENHANCE THE CAPACITY OF TARGETED GOVERNMENT OF NEPAL MINISTRIES AND AGENCIES TO IMPROVE TRANSPARENCY AND ACCOUNTABILITY OF PUBLIC FINANCIAL MANAGEMENT SYSTEMS. PFMSP WILL SUPPORT THE GONS CAPACITY BUILDING PRIORITIES, WHICH HAVE BEEN IDENTIFIED THROUGH EXTENSIVE CONSULTATIONS BETWEEN USAID, GON AND THE DONOR COMMUNITY ENGAGED IN PFM REFORM EFFORTS.    THE EXPECTED RESULTS OF PFMSP ARE LINKED TO THE DGO WITH COUNTRY DEVELOOPMENT COOPERATION STRATEGY RESULTS FRAMEWORK AS FOLLOWS IR 1 IMPROVED BUDGET PLANNING AND EXECUTION CAPACITY OF GON IR 2 IMPROVED CONTROL ENVIRONMENT AND PROCUREMENT CAPACITY IN TARGETED MINISTRIES AND OR AGENCIES IR 3 STRENGTHENED FINANCIAL MANAGEMENT FUNCTIONS OF NATIONAL AUTHROITY FOR RECONSTRUCTION   COMPONENTS  TO ACHIEVE THE GOAL AND RESULTS, THE CONTRACTOR MUST IMPLEMENT ACTIVITIES ALIGNED WITH THE FOLLOWING THREE PROJECT COMPONENTS  COMPONENT A  STRENGTHEN THE LINE MINISTRY BUDGETARY MANAGEMENT INFORMATION SYSTEM.   COMPONENT B  INSTITUTIONALIZE CONTROL ENVIRONMENT AND IMPROVED PROCUREMENT PRACTICES ACROSS SELECTED LINE  MINISTRIES AND AGENCIES.  COMPONENT C  SUPPORT THE GONS EFFORTS TO INCREASE FINANCIAL TRANSPARENCY AND ACCOUNTABILITY OF POST EARTHQUAKE RECONSTRUCTION AND RECOVERY ASSISTANCE.
USAID/NEPAL EARLY GRADE LEARNING (EGL) ACTIVITY AIM TO STRENGTHEN THE CAPACITY OF FEDERAL, PROVINCIAL, AND MUNICIPAL GOVERNMENTS TO IMPROVE LEARNING OUTCOMES IN GRADES 1-3.

USAID/NEPAL WILL USE THE PRODUCTS AND DELIVERABLES FROM THIS CONTRACT TO (A) FACILITATE INFORMED PROGRAM MANAGEMENT, (B) SHAPE THE LONGER-TERM STRATEGIC DIRECTION OF PROGRAMS AND DECISION-MAKING WITHIN THE MISSION, AND (C) ENABLE USAID/NEPAL TO COMPLY WITH AGENCY ACCOUNTABILITY AND M&E REQUIREMENTS. THE OVERALL GOAL OF THE POPULATION BASED SURVEY (PBS) IS TO  PROVIDE USAID/NEPAL, ITS U.S. GOVERNMENT INTER-AGENCY  PARTNERS. THE USAID BUREAU FOR FOOD SECURITY, USAID MISSIONS,  THE GOVERNMENT OF NEPAL, AND DEVELOPMENT PARTNERS WITH  INFORMATION ON THE CURRENT STATUS OF THE FEED THE FUTURE  POPULATION-BASED ZOI LEVEL INDICATORS IN NEPAL. THE SURVEY IS  DESIGNED TO (1) DETERMINE WHETHER THERE HAS BEEN STATISTICALLY  SIGNIFICANT CHANGE OVER TIME IN THE FEED THE FUTURE PHASE ONE  ZOI KEY OUTCOME AND IMPACT INDICATROS, AND (2) ESTABLISH THE  BASELINE STATUS OF FEED THE FUTURE PHASE TWO ZOI INDICATORS.  THEREFORE, THE 2018 NEPAL ZOI SURVEY IS INTENDED TO SERVE BOTH  AS AN ENDLINE FOR FEED THE FUTURE PHASE ONE AND A BASELINE FOR  FEED THE FUTURE PHASE TWO IN NEPAL.

TO AWARD A NEW USPSC FROM JUNE 14, 2014 TO JUNE 13, 2016 (TWO YEARS). ADDITIONAL RESPONSIBILITIES. SHE NOW SUPERVISES, AND HAS BEEN ACTIVELY INVOLVED IN PURSUING BOTH A NEW VISION FOR CRS AND A SOUND BUSINESS PLAN FOR THE PROGRAM INCOME THE ORGANIZATION GENERATES. SHE CONTINUES TO ACTIVELY MENTOR AN EMERGING LEADER ON THE HFP TEAM, AND HAS WORKED TO PREPARE HER TO SERVE AS AGREEMENT OFFICER REPRESENTATIVE (AOR) FOR THE PENDING SEBAC LL COOPERATIVE AGREEMENT. HAS ALSO LED USAID/S EFFORTS TO INTEGRATE WATER, SANITATION AND HYGIENE (WASH) INTO THE NUTRITION PORTFOLIO FOR MAXIMUM EFFECTIVENESS. IN ACCORDANCE WITH USAID'S INITIATIVE TO COMPLY WITH UNITED STATES GOVERNMENT (USG) REQUIREMENTS FOR AORS, RE-CERTIFIED AS AN AOR IN MARCH 2014. WELCOMED AS A LEARNING EXPERIENCE, THIS TRAINING WILL UPDATE EXTENSIVE KNOWLEDGE OF USA ID RULES AND REGULATIONS IN A WAY THAT WILL BENEFIT THE ENTIRE HFP TEAM. IN THE UPCOMING YEARS, WILL CONTINUE TO LEND HER CONSIDERABLE EXPERTISE IN WASH TO THE HFP TEAM. FURTHERMORE, SHE WILL CONTINUE TO LEAD FROM BEHIND GROWS INTO HER NEW RESPONSIBILITIES. FINALLY, WILL CONTINUE TO PROVIDE SUBJECT-MATTER EXPERTISE TO A VARIETY OF PROJECTS THAT SHE MANAGES AS AN ACTIVITY MANAGER, FROM VACCINATION EFFORTS IN COORDINATION WITH THE WORLD HEALTH ORGANIZATION, TO ASSISTING NEPALIS WHO HAVE PHYSICAL DISABILITIES AS PART OF THE STRIDE ACTIVITY, TO COUNTERING NEW AND EMERGING PANDEMIC THREATS SUCH AS ZOONOTIC DISEASES AND INFLUENZA. messages: For searches, time period start and end dates are currently limited to an earliest date of 2007-10-01.  For data going back to 2000-10-01, use either the Custom Award Download feature on the website or one of our download or bulk_download API endpoints as listed on https://api.usaspending.gov/docs/endpoints.
